summary: Decompiled most of the common fighter item-swing block around ft_0CD1/ft_0CD3, splitting the original ft_0CD1 object at 0x800CD350 so the new ft_0CD3.c unit could be marked Matching. The PR added shared swing helpers, sword/bat/parasol/harisen swing callbacks, Harisen item interaction calls, motion variable fields for common swing state, and several header/type corrections needed to express the code cleanly. decomp.dev reported 27 new matches and +1696 bytes of matched code, with ftCo_800CD278 improved but still only 98.95% matched.
what_changed: Filled in ft_0CD1.c with common swing helper/control functions from 0x800CD140 through 0x800CD31C, including motion-state entry, animation end handling, IASA A-button/throw flag handling, physics dispatch, collision-to-fall handling, and item-loss damage callback handling.
Added new source file src/melee/ft/ft_0CD3.c for the 0x800CD350-0x800CD82C region, covering sword, bat, parasol, and harisen swing entry/anim/IASA/phys/coll wrappers.
Moved the object split in config/GALE01/splits.txt so ft_0CD1.c now ends at 0x800CD350 and new ft_0CD3.c covers 0x800CD350-0x800CD82C, with matching sdata2 ranges adjusted.
Added ft_0CD3.c as a Matching object in configure.py while ft_0CD1.c remained NonMatching.
Changed ftCo_800CD140's declaration and call sites to include a float argument, used as the motion-state speed/rate parameter in Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
Added mv.co.swing fields x0, x4, and x8 to ftCommon_MotionVars to represent common swing state used by the matched code.
Refined ftCommonData field x420 from UNK_T to float, enabling a matched expression using p_ftCommonData->x420 * fp->co_attrs.gr_friction in ftCo_800CD278.
Replaced several itharisen.h UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with concrete void signatures taking Item_GObj* and, for animation-speed functions, float parameters.
Updated ftstarrodswing.c to pass the new float argument through to ftCo_800CD140.
smart_moves: Separated the previously broad ft_0CD1 object into ft_0CD1.c and ft_0CD3.c so the later contiguous block could be marked Matching independently while the small remaining mismatch in ft_0CD1 did not block progress.

Centralized repeated item-swing behavior through shared helper functions such as ftCo_800CD1BC, ftCo_800CD204, ftCo_800CD278, and ftCo_800CD2C4, then implemented sword/bat/parasol/harisen callbacks as thin wrappers where the assembly evidently did the same.
Introduced a dedicated mv.co.swing struct in the common motion-vars union instead of scattering raw offsets, making repeated state fields fp->mv.co.swing.x0/x4/x8 visible to future decomp work.
Tightened external Harisen item prototypes only as far as the new call sites demonstrated: Item_GObj* parameters and float frame-speed parameters, with no invented semantic names.

Preserved unknown function-style names where semantics were not established, while using known action names for obvious callbacks such as ftCo_SwordSwing_Anim, ftCo_BatSwing_IASA, and ftCo_HarisenSwing_Coll.
decomp_lessons: For partially matched regions, object splitting can convert a large nonmatching block into a smaller nonmatching prefix plus a fully matching new object, improving project metrics without forcing every nearby function to match at once.

When a helper signature is wrong, fix the header and all dependent call sites together; here ftCo_800CD140 needed a float argument because its implementation passes that value into Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
Repeated Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll action callbacks are often tiny tail/wrapper functions around shared common helpers; matching these benefits from identifying the shared helper first.

Motion variable union fields should be added when multiple functions consistently read/write the same offsets, but placeholder field names like x0/x4/x8 are acceptable when semantics remain uncertain.
Concrete item and common-data prototypes can unblock matching by giving the compiler the right argument passing and float operations, even before final semantic names are known.
Callback fields such as take_dmg_cb and deal_dmg_cb are important matching evidence in fighter item-swing states; the PR used different callbacks for ordinary swings versus Harisen behavior.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action functions use the ftCo_ prefix, e.g. ftCo_SwordSwing_Anim, ftCo_BatSwing_Phys, ftCo_ParasolSwing_Coll, and ftCo_HarisenSwing_IASA.
Unknown-address functions retained address-based names such as ftCo_800CD140, ftCo_800CD5A8, ft_800CD604, and ft_800CD65C.
Motion callback suffixes follow the established action-state pattern: _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, and _Coll.
Item Harisen functions retain address-based it_8029xxxx names where their exact semantic names are not known.
New common motion variables were grouped under mv.co.swing with placeholder x0, x4, and x8 field names rather than over-naming uncertain fields.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used a new split boundary at 0x800CD350 to isolate a matching subunit, with ft_0CD3.c added as Object(Matching, "melee/ft/ft_0CD3.c") and ft_0CD1.c left NonMatching.
Adjusted sdata2 ranges alongside the text split: ft_0CD1.c uses 0x804D8EF8-0x804D8EFC and ft_0CD3.c uses 0x804D8F00-0x804D8F08.
Passed constants through the shared entry helper to match per-item variants, including arg2 flag values 0 and 0x400 in ftCo_800CD140 calls.

Matched Harisen swing animation behavior by gating item calls on fp->item_gobj, using cmd_vars[0] values 1 and 2, switching on mv.co.swing.x4, and passing fp->frame_speed_mul to the Harisen item functions.
Used p_ftCommonData->x5A0 as a timer/reset value for mv.co.swing.x8 after deal_dmg_cb and counted it down by 1.0f in ftCo_HarisenSwing_Anim.
Used callback function pointers in collision helper ftCo_800CD2C4 so different swing kinds can run item cleanup before entering fall.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
decomp.dev reported matched code improved to 43.01% (+0.04%, +1696 bytes) and linked code to 27.34% (+0.03%, +1244 bytes).
decomp.dev reported 27 new fully matched functions, mostly in main/melee/ft/ft_0CD3 plus several in ft_0CD1.
decomp.dev reported one remaining partial improvement: ftCo_800CD278 in main/melee/ft/ft_0CD1 improved to 98.95% rather than 100%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching ftCo_800CD278 or rename mv.co.swing.x0/x4/x8.
Search for later semantic renames of ft_800CD604 and ft_800CD65C Harisen callbacks.
Check whether ft_0CD1.c was later marked Matching after resolving the 98.95% ftCo_800CD278 mismatch.
Look for PRs refining itharisen.h address-based function names into semantic names.
